title: GitHub push protection, secret scanning, and main branch protection not yet enabled
affects: Compensating control for secret_scan.py regex false negatives (M0-T005 G5 review F2/F3)
any task that handles a real credential
detail: docs/SECRETS_POLICY.md section 6 designates GitHub push protection + secret scanning as the server-side compensating control for the inherent false negatives of the repo-local regex scanner. The M0-T005 G5 security review (findings F2, F3) requires this to be tracked as a human action with a blocker record, and also asks the owner to confirm branch protection on main (mitigates the scanner-is-self-modifiable-from-a-PR residual risk). Nothing is blocked today; this becomes critical before the first real credential is handled.
exact_human_action: In https://github.com/martin10101/nyc-buildability/settings/security_analysis enable Secret scanning and Push protection (if the private-repo plan does not include them, report that so a documented exception can be recorded). In branch settings, confirm or add protection on main requiring pull requests and passing checks.
how_it_will_be_tested: Owner confirmation or settings screenshot; optionally a rejected test push containing a canary GitHub-format token from a scratch branch.

audit_log.note: RESOLVED: repository public as of 2026-07-20; orchestrator enabled secret_scanning=enabled and secret_scanning_push_protection=enabled via the repository API (PATCH security_and_analysis, response confirmed both enabled). Main branch protection now enforced server-side by ruleset protect-main (see B-008 resolution). Canary-token rejection test deliberately NOT performed (would require crafting a token-shaped string; the API state confirmation is the acceptance evidence). NOTE: repo is now public - the legally-sensitive-content consideration was owner-decided in favor of public.
